watchFor: AVOID: Feb 11: 3 trades, 0 wins, -$4,757 — this is a full wipeout day with no stops honored or no exit from losing positions early enough
AVOID: Feb 13: After a -$7,627 two-day hole, still traded and lost another -$2,157 — revenge trading or failure to step away
AVOID: Win rate of 37.9% is below 40% threshold for sustainability unless average winner is 2.5x+ average loser — this ratio needs to be calculated and confirmed
AVOID: Extended inactivity (5 days) without a documented re-entry plan is not a strategy, it is avoidance
tomorrowRules: Define and write down your re-entry trigger before the open: e.g., 'I will take maximum 2 trades tomorrow, size at 50% normal, and stop at -$1,000 total'
If market regime is NEUTRAL or RISK_OFF, reduce position size by 50% — do not trade full size until RISK_ON confirmed with VIX below 18
Hard daily loss limit of $1,800 (2% of account). If hit, platform closes, no exceptions — this rule must have been violated on 2/10 and 2/11
After any loss, wait 15 minutes before taking the next trade — mandatory cooling period
Calculate your actual average winner vs. average loser ratio from the 29-trade sample and post it somewhere visible before trading

dailyLessons.gradingReason: No trades today which avoids further losses, but five consecutive zero-trade days suggest either excessive caution or system/data issues. Cumulative account is profitable at +$11,947 but the 37.9% win rate with recent heavy losing days (-$4,757 on 2/11, -$2,870 on 2/10, -$2,157 on 2/13) indicates a serious risk management problem when active. Sitting out a NEUTRAL market regime with VIX at manageable levels is defensible, not heroic.
keyLessons: Five straight days of zero trades after a brutal losing streak (2/10 through 2/13 lost ~$10,482) suggests you may be in a confidence freeze — this is a common but dangerous response. Define explicit re-entry criteria so you return by rule, not by feeling.

Your cumulative win rate of 37.9% means your winners must be significantly larger than your losers to be profitable. The +$20,632 on 2/6 is masking the damage. Do not confuse a lucky large winner with a working system.

The Feb 10-13 drawdown of ~$10,482 across 4 trading days on a ~$90k account is an 11.6% drawdown in under a week. That violates standard risk discipline. Maximum daily loss limits (e.g., 2% of equity = ~$1,800/day) must be enforced before you return to active trading.

goodPatterns: Stopped trading after a significant losing streak — prevented further emotional damage during a choppy RISK_OFF market regime
Swing account appears stable and separately managed, showing some diversification discipline
Overall account remains profitable despite recent losses, suggesting the system has edge when properly controlled

Win rate of 37.9% is below 40% threshold for sustainability unless average winner is 2.5x+ average loser — this ratio needs to be calculated and confirmed
Extended inactivity (5 days) without a documented re-entry plan is not a strategy, it is avoidance

dailyLessons.riskAssessment: Risk management today: N/A (no trades). Historical risk management: Poor. The Feb 10-13 losing streak totaling approximately -$10,482 across 4 days represents an ~11.6% account drawdown with no apparent circuit breakers engaged. The -$4,757 single-day loss on 2/11 alone is catastrophic risk management. A profitable cumulative balance masks a system that can catastrophically self-destruct without hard daily loss limits. Fix this before resuming active trading.
dailyLessons.emotionalCheck: High probability of a confidence freeze response to the Feb 10-13 losses. Trading continued into 2/13 after two consecutive heavy loss days (classic revenge or recency bias), then went to zero activity for 5+ days (classic shutdown response). Neither extreme is healthy. The pattern — overtrading through losses, then full stop — is a textbook emotional cycle. The remedy is rules-based circuit breakers that prevent both extremes: a daily loss limit stops the revenge trading, and a pre-written re-entry protocol prevents the paralysis.
